title: Fluentd Input-to-Output Throughput Mismatch Detection
dataSources: `index=fluent` `sourcetype=fluentd:plugins` `in_*` and `out_*` emit counters on same host
app: Fluentd monitoring agent JSON, 60s poll to Splunk
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:plugins"
| eval vol=coalesce(emit_records, emit_count, 0)
| bin _time span=1m
| stats sum(eval(if(match(plugin_type,"^in"),vol,0))) as vin, sum(eval(if(match(plugin_type,"^out"),vol,0))) as vout by _time, host
| eval gap=abs(vin-vout)
| where vin > 1000 AND gap > vin * 0.25
| sort -gap
description: Sustained divergence between ingress and egress counters means filters are dropping unexpectedly, buffers are stalling, or metrics scraping is incomplete. The gap metric catches subtle pipeline starvation before disk buffers fill.
value: Protects log completeness SLAs and accelerates root cause during partial outages.
implementation: Use `eventstats` to pivot in→out per host if `xyseries` is awkward: `stats sum(eval(if(dir="in",vol,null))) as vin ...` then `eval gap=abs(vin-vout)`. Align poll intervals; both legs must share timestamps.
visualization: Dual-axis timechart (in vs out), gap histogram, table of worst hosts.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how we use and protect smart assistant and model calls so cost, quality, and misuse stay in a range we are willing to own.
knownFalsePositives: Buffer pain from downstream saturation, bad TLS, or local disk. We check agent and destination before blind buffer tuning.
